title: Lady M----ve (Sketches In The Exhibition, 1805)
content:
How clear a strife of light and shade is spread!
    The face how touched with nature's loveliest red!
    The eye, how eloquent, and yet how meek!
    The glow subdued, yet mantling on thy cheek!
    M----ve! I mark alone thy beauteous face,
    But all is nature, dignity, and grace!
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Lisle Bowles, titled "Lady M----ve (Sketches In The Exhibition, 1805)", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
